If \(n\) is a positive integer, then \(3^2 \cdot 4^n=\)
A. \(6^{2n}\).
B. \(6^n\).
C. \(12^n\).
D. \(12^{n+?}\).

Let \(x\) be the smaller one of two consecutive integers. If the sum of the squares of the two integers is less than three times the product of the two integers by \(1\), then
A. \(x^2+(x+1)^2=3x(x+1)-1\).
B. \(x^2+(x+1)^2=3x(x+1)+1\).
C. \(3\bigl(x^2+(x+1)^2\bigr)=x(x+1)-1\).
D. \(3\bigl(x^2+(x+1)^2\bigr)=x(x+1)+1\).

Which of the following statements about the graph of \(y=(x+1)(x-3)\) is true?
A. The coordinates of the vertex of the graph are \((-1,4)\)
B. The equation of the axis of symmetry of the graph is \(x=1\)
C. The \(x\)-intercepts of the graph are \(-1\) and \(3\)
D. The \(y\)-intercept of the graph is \(-3\)

The solution of \(15 \ge 4(x+2)-1\) is
A. \(x\le -2\).
B. \(x\le 2\).
C. \(x\ge -2\).
D. \(x\ge 2\).

The price of \(6\) oranges and \(3\) apples is \(\$42\) while the price of \(8\) oranges and \(5\) apples is \(\$60\). Find the price of an apple.
A. \(\$3\)
B. \(\$4\)
C. \(\$5\)
D. \(\$6\)

In the figure, the \(1\)st pattern consists of \(4\) dots. For any positive integer \(n\), the \((n+1)\)th pattern is formed by adding \(4\) dots to the \(n\)th pattern. Find the number of dots in the \(9\)th pattern.

A. \(36\)
B. \(40\)
C. \(81\)
D. \(100\)

If the bus fare is increased from \(\$4\) to \(\$5\), then the percentage increase of the fare is
A. \(20\%\).
B. \(25\%\).
C. \(80\%\).
D. \(125\%\).

A sum of \(\$30000\) is deposited at an interest rate of \(12\%\) per annum for \(4\) years, compounded monthly. Find the amount correct to the nearest dollar.
A. \(\$44400\)
B. \(\$47206\)
C. \(\$48141\)
D. \(\$48367\)

Express \(\sqrt{2007}\) as a decimal correct to \(5\) significant figures.
A. \(44.750\)
B. \(44.799\)
C. \(44.75955\)
D. \(44.800\)

It is given that \(y\) is partly constant and partly varies directly as \(x\). When \(x=2\), \(y=17\) and when \(x=4\), \(y=11\). Find the value of \(x\) when \(y=5\).
A. \(-3\)
B. \(6\)
C. \(8\)
D. \(112\)

\(A\) and \(B\) are two points on a map. If the bearing of \(A\) from \(B\) is \(110^\circ\), then the bearing of \(B\) from \(A\) is
A. \(070^\circ\).
B. \(250^\circ\).
C. \(290^\circ\).
D. \(340^\circ\).

In the figure, \(OAB\) is a sector with centre \(O\). If the perimeter of the sector \(OAB\) is \(12\text{ cm}\), find \(OA\) correct to the nearest \(0.01\text{ cm}\).

A. \(3.36\text{ cm}\)
B. \(3.91\text{ cm}\)
C. \(4.31\text{ cm}\)
D. \(7.64\text{ cm}\)

In the figure, the volume of the right prism is

A. \(456\text{ cm}^3\).
B. \(540\text{ cm}^3\).
C. \(552\text{ cm}^3\).
D. \(636\text{ cm}^3\).

If a solid metal hemisphere of radius \(r\) is melted and recast into \(3\) identical solid right circular cones of height \(h\) and base radius \(r\), then \(r:h=\)
A. \(2:3\).
B. \(3:2\).
C. \(3:4\).
D. \(4:3\).

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a parallelogram. \(E\) is a point lying on \(AB\). If \(EC\) and \(BD\) intersect at \(F\), then the ratio of the area of \(\Delta DEF\) to the area of \(\Delta CBF\) is

A. \(1:1\).
B. \(1:2\).
C. \(2:1\).
D. \(2:3\).

If \(x\) and \(y\) are acute angles such that \(x+y=90^\circ\), which of the following must be true?
I. \(\sin x=\cos y\)
II. \(\sin(90^\circ-x)=\cos(90^\circ-y)\)
III. \(\tan x \tan y=1\)
A. I and II only.
B. I and III only.
C. II and III only.
D. I, II and III.

\(\dfrac{\cos A}{\sin A}+\dfrac{\sin A}{\cos A}=\)
A. \(1\).
B. \(1+\tan^2A\).
C. \(\sin A\cos A\).
D. \(\dfrac{1}{\sin A\cos A}\).

Solve the equation \(\sin\theta=\sqrt{3}\cos\theta\), where \(0^\circ\le\theta\le90^\circ\).
A. \(\theta=0^\circ\)
B. \(\theta=30^\circ\)
C. \(\theta=45^\circ\)
D. \(\theta=60^\circ\)

In the figure, \(ABC\) is a right-angled triangle. \(BD\) is the angle bisector of \(\angle ABC\). If \(AB=c\), then \(CD=\)

A. \(\dfrac{c}{\sqrt{3}}\).
B. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}c\).
C. \(\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{4}c\).
D. \(\dfrac{c}{4}\).

In the figure, \(ABCDE\) is a right pyramid with the square base \(BCDE\). \(F\) is a point lying on \(AC\) such that \(BF\) and \(DF\) are perpendicular to \(AC\). The angle between the plane \(ABC\) and the plane \(ACD\) is

A. \(\angle ACB\).
B. \(\angle BAD\).
C. \(\angle BCD\).
D. \(\angle BFD\).

Which of the following plane figures have rotational symmetry?

A. I and II only
B. I and III only
C. II and III only
D. I, II and III

In the figure, the square \(ABCD\) is divided into nine identical squares and four of them are shaded. The number of axes of reflectional symmetry of the square \(ABCD\) is

A. \(2\).
B. \(4\).
C. \(5\).
D. \(8\).

If the sum of the interior angles of a convex \(n\)-sided polygon is \(4\) times the sum of the exterior angles of the polygon, then \(n=\)
A. \(4\).
B. \(6\).
C. \(8\).
D. \(10\).

In the figure, \(AY\) and \(CY\) are the angle bisectors of \(\angle BAX\) and \(\angle DCX\) respectively. If \(\angle AXC=100^\circ\), then \(\angle AYC=\)

A. \(40^\circ\).
B. \(50^\circ\).
C. \(60^\circ\).
D. \(80^\circ\).

If the point \((3,2)\) is rotated clockwise about the origin through \(90^\circ\), then the coordinates of its image are
A. \((2,3)\).
B. \((3,2)\).
C. \((-2,-3)\).
D. \((-3,-2)\).

If the rectangular coordinates of the point \(A\) are \((1,1)\), then the polar coordinates of \(A\) are
A. \((1,135^\circ)\).
B. \((1,225^\circ)\).
C. \((\sqrt{2},135^\circ)\).
D. \((\sqrt{2},225^\circ)\).

Find the equation of the straight line which is perpendicular to the straight line \(x+2y+3=0\) and passes through the point \((1,3)\).
A. \(x+2y-7=0\)
B. \(x-2y+5=0\)
C. \(2x+y-5=0\)
D. \(2x-y+1=0\)

The figure shows the graph of the straight line \(ax+by+1=0\). Which of the following is true?

A. \(a\gt0\) and \(b\gt0\)
B. \(a\gt0\) and \(b\lt0\)
C. \(a\lt0\) and \(b\gt0\)
D. \(a\lt0\) and \(b\lt0\)

Two numbers are randomly drawn at the same time from five cards numbered \(1,2,3,4\) and \(5\) respectively. Find the probability that the sum of the numbers drawn is a multiple of \(3\).
A. \(\dfrac{2}{5}\)
B. \(\dfrac{3}{5}\)
C. \(\dfrac{9}{20}\)
D. \(\dfrac{9}{25}\)

If the mode of the seven numbers \(8,7,1,3,7,a\) and \(b\) is \(8\), then the median of the seven numbers is
A. \(3\).
B. \(6\).
C. \(7\).
D. \(8\).

\(B_X\) and \(B_Y\) are the box-and-whisker diagrams for the distributions \(X\) and \(Y\) respectively.

Let \(\mu_1\), \(q_1\) and \(r_1\) be the mean, the interquartile range and the range of \(X\) respectively while \(\mu_2\), \(q_2\) and \(r_2\) are the mean, the interquartile range and the range of \(Y\) respectively. Which of the following must be true?
I. \(\mu_1\lt\mu_2\)
II. \(q_1\lt q_2\)
III. \(r_1\lt r_2\)
A. I only.
B. II only.
C. I and III only.
D. II and III only.

The stem-and-leaf diagram below shows the distribution of the weights (in kg) of some students.

Which of the following frequency curves may represent the distribution of the weights?
A.
B.
C.
D.

Which of the following may represent the graph of \(y=f(x)\) and the graph of \(y=f(x+1)\) on the same rectangular coordinate system?

A
B
C
D

Let \(f(x)\) be a polynomial. If \(f(x)\) is divisible by \(x-1\), which of the following must be a factor of \(f(2x+1)\)?
A. \(x\)
B. \(x-3\)
C. \(2x-1\)
D. \(2x+1\)

Which of the regions in the figure may represent the solution of
\[\begin{aligned}y &\ge 4,\\x+y &\le 8,\\2x+y &\ge 8?\end{aligned}\]
A. Region I.
B. Region II.
C. Region III.
D. Region IV.

Let \(a_n\) be the \(n\)th term of an arithmetic sequence. If \(a_7=a_1-6\) and \(a_1+a_2+\cdots+a_{22}=1624\), then \(a_1=\)
A. \(-52\).
B. \(-26\).
C. \(-23\).
D. \(139\).

The sum of all the positive terms in the geometric sequence \(4,-2,1,\ldots\) is
A. \(8\).
B. \(\infty\).
C. \(\dfrac{16}{3}\).
D. \(\dfrac{16}{5}\).

Let \(k\) be a constant and \(-90^\circ\lt\theta\lt90^\circ\). If the figure shows the graph of \(y=k\sin(x^\circ+\theta)\), then

A. \(k=-2\) and \(\theta=-30^\circ\).
B. \(k=-2\) and \(\theta=30^\circ\).
C. \(k=2\) and \(\theta=-30^\circ\).
D. \(k=2\) and \(\theta=30^\circ\).

In the figure, find \(x\) correct to the nearest integer.

A. \(14\)
B. \(15\)
C. \(16\)
D. \(17\)

In the figure, \(A\), \(B\), \(C\) and \(D\) are points lying on the circle. If \(AB=5\), \(AD=3\) and \(BD=7\), then \(\angle BCD=\)

A. \(60^\circ\).
B. \(85^\circ\).
C. \(95^\circ\).
D. \(120^\circ\).

In the figure, \(A\), \(B\) and \(C\) are points lying on the circle. \(AB\) is a diameter of the circle. \(DB\) is the tangent to the circle at \(B\). If \(ACD\) is a straight line with \(AC=4\) and \(CD=2\), then \(AB=\)

A. \(2\sqrt{6}\).
B. \(4\sqrt{3}\).
C. \(4\sqrt{6}\).
D. \(8\sqrt{3}\).

If \(\Delta ABC\) is an obtuse-angled triangle, which of the following points must lie outside \(\Delta ABC\)?
I. The centroid of \(\Delta ABC\)
II. The circumcentre of \(\Delta ABC\)
III. The orthocentre of \(\Delta ABC\)
A. I and II only.
B. I and III only.
C. II and III only.
D. I, II and III.

A circle \(C\) touches the \(y\)-axis. If the coordinates of the centre of \(C\) are \((3,4)\), then the equation of \(C\) is
A. \((x-3)^2+(y+4)^2=9\).
B. \((x-3)^2+(y+4)^2=16\).
C. \((x+3)^2+(y-4)^2=9\).
D. \((x+3)^2+(y-1)^2=-16\).

Let \(a\) be a constant. If the circle \(x^2+y^2+ax-6y-3=0\) passes through the point \((2,3)\), then the area of the circle is
A. \(8\pi\).
B. \(10\pi\).
C. \(16\pi\).
D. \(55\pi\).

A bag contains \(8\) black balls and \(5\) white balls. If two balls are drawn randomly from the bag one by one without replacement, then the probability that the two balls are of the same colour is
A. \(\dfrac{14}{39}\).
B. \(\dfrac{19}{39}\).
C. \(\dfrac{89}{156}\).
D. \(\dfrac{89}{169}\).

One letter is chosen randomly from each of the two words CUBE and CONE . Find the probability that the two letters chosen are different.
A. \(\dfrac{1}{4}\)
B. \(\dfrac{3}{4}\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{8}\)
D. \(\dfrac{7}{8}\)
